brief_justification: Paper A is an empirical market-microstructure study: it econometrically estimates Kyle's price-impact coefficient from equity order flow (regression, Amihud ratio) and runs Fama-MacBeth cross-sectional forecasts with Newey-West adjustment to explain the liquidity premium via adverse selection. Paper B analytically/numerically solves a nonlinear gauge-field (quartic F^4) equation in a fixed AdS4 background, deriving a first integral and constitutive relation and then computing holographic retarded Green's functions and fermion spectral weight. The only overlap is the incidental symbol lambda/coupling; there is no shared governing process (statistical estimation from data vs. solving a nonlinear PDE in a holographic bulk), so no role-for-role mapping holds.
